com.mentor.chs.plugin.user

Interface IXUser

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXObject

    public interface IXUser
extends IXObject
    Class exposing typed holder for a User.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      java.lang.String getChangePolicy() 
      java.util.Set<? extends IXDomain> getDomains() 
      java.lang.String getFullName() 
      java.util.Set<? extends IXRole> getRoles() 
      java.lang.String getUserName() 
      boolean isDomainWritable(java.lang.String domainName)
      Domain name is checked against all writable domains of the user.If its found true is returned indicating the domain is writable.

      • Methods inherited from interface com.mentor.chs.api.IXObject

        getAttribute, getAttributes, getBaseID, getID, getParentID, getProperties, getProperty, getSymbol, toHTML, toHTML

    • Method Detail

      • getUserName

        java.lang.String getUserName()
        Returns:
        The user name (login name)

      • getFullName

        java.lang.String getFullName()
        Returns:
        The Full name of the user

      • getRoles

        java.util.Set<? extends IXRole> getRoles()
        Returns:
        The Roles assigned to the user

      • getDomains

        java.util.Set<? extends IXDomain> getDomains()
        Returns:
        The Domains assigned to the user

      • getChangePolicy

        java.lang.String getChangePolicy()
        Returns:
        The Name of the Change Policy associated with the user

      • isDomainWritable

        boolean isDomainWritable(java.lang.String domainName)
        Domain name is checked against all writable domains of the user.If its found true is returned indicating the domain is writable. If the domain name is null or empty it returns true. The API does a case sensitive match of domain name
        Parameters:
        domainName - Domain Name to check if its a writable domain or not
        Returns:
        If user has a write permission on domain or not
